content: Creatinine clearance estimation: Cockcroft-Gault equation: CrCl = [(140-age) × weight(kg)] / (72 × SCr) × 0.85 (if female). Use actual body weight unless obese (>120% ideal body weight), then use adjusted body weight. CKD-EPI equation more accurate for eGFR >60. Drug dosing studies historically used Cockcroft-Gault; FDA now accepts CKD-EPI. Therapeutic drug monitoring essential for narrow therapeutic index renally-cleared drugs: vancomycin trough 10-20 mcg/mL; gentamicin peak 6-10, trough <2 mcg/mL; digoxin 0.5-0.9 ng/mL; lithium 0.6-1.2 mEq/L. Acute kidney injury requires daily reassessment of renal function and dose adjustment. Augmented renal clearance (CrCl >130) in critically ill may require higher doses.
